title: Drain cleaning powder
subtitle: A fizzing drain treatment with bicarbonate of soda, washing soda, and citric acid
excerpt: A dry powder drain treatment made from bicarbonate of soda, washing soda, and citric acid. Pour down the drain, follow with a kettle of very hot water, and let it fizz for 15 minutes before flushing. Effective for deodorising and clearing minor build-up of soap scum and organic debris. Not a replacement for clearing a blockage mechanically.
sourceNotes: Dri-Pak washing soda for drains (dri-pak.co.uk, accessed May 2026): washing soda dissolves soap scum and grease in drains. Acid-base fizz mechanism for drain cleaning.

content.text: A monthly drain treatment keeps drains smelling fresh. It also slows the build-up of soap scum and gunk that can lead to a blockage. The
comes from the citric acid and bicarbonate of soda reacting with water. The washing soda then does the heavy cleaning on grease and soap residue.
maintenance treatment, not a blockage clearer

. If the drain is blocked, remove the obstruction mechanically first. Wear protective gloves when making and using the powder; the washing soda is alkaline enough to irritate skin. Keep the dry powder away from any moisture source.

items.name: Bicarbonate of soda
items.prepNote: Plain bicarbonate of soda. Provides the fizzing action and mild alkaline base.

items.name: Washing soda
items.prepNote: Sodium carbonate. The primary degreasing agent. Wear protective gloves when handling.

items.name: Citric acid
items.prepNote: Fine citric acid granules. Reacts with the bicarbonate to produce the fizzing action when water is added.

content.text: Wearing protective gloves, weigh the bicarbonate of soda, washing soda, and citric acid into the dry mixing bowl. Stir briefly to combine. Transfer to a sealed glass jar and label with the making date. Store completely away from moisture.

Pour 80 to 100 g of the powder directly into the drain. Follow immediately with a full kettle of very hot water poured slowly down the drain. The powder will fizz vigorously. Leave for 15 minutes, then flush with cold water. Use once a month as a preventative treatment.

Keep in a sealed glass jar in a dry cupboard. The powder keeps for 12 months if stored completely dry. Any clumping, fizzing in the jar, or change of texture means moisture has entered. Discard and remake. Do not store in a bathroom cabinet where steam and humidity are regular.

recipe.yieldDescription: ~350 g dry powder (3 to 4 drain treatments)
